info.name: BarterX API - Complete Collection
info.description: Complete API collection for BarterX barter marketplace

request.description: Register a new user with JSON body (existing flow). No avatar upload.

formdata.description: Profile picture (optional). Max 5MB. Formats: JPG, PNG, GIF, WebP

request.description: Register a new user with form-data including optional avatar upload. Avatar will be resized to 400x400 and uploaded to barterx/avatars folder. If avatar upload fails, user is still registered but a warning is returned.
Retrieve a user's public profile by their ID. Returns name, avatar, location, rating, active listings count, and isIdVerified status.

formdata.description: Government-issued ID document image. Accepted formats: JPEG, PNG, WebP. Max size: 5MB. Stored in barterx/id-documents/ folder.

request.description:
Upload a government-issued ID document (passport, driver's licence, national ID) to initiate the identity verification flow.

**Prerequisites:** User must have a verified email address (isEmailVerified = true).

**Behaviour:**
- Accepts multipart/form-data with field name `idDocument`.
- Stores the image in `barterx/id-documents/` on Cloudinary (or local fallback).
- Sets `idDocumentUrl` on the user's profile.
- Resets `isIdVerified` to `false`, `idVerifiedAt` and `idVerifiedBy` to `null` (re-upload always requires admin to re-verify).

**Next step:** Admin reviews the document and calls `PATCH /api/admin/users/:id/verify` to approve.
Validation test: Submitting the form without attaching a file should return HTTP 400 with message 'No ID document file provided'.

item.name: Upload ID Document - Wrong Field Name (400)

exec: pm.test('Returns 400 for wrong form field name', () => {

request.description: Validation test: File uploaded under field name `document` instead of `idDocument`. Multer will ignore it and the handler returns 400.

item.name: Upload ID Document - Invalid File Type (400)

formdata.description: Attach a PDF or non-image file here to trigger the file type rejection

request.description: Validation test: Uploading a PDF, GIF, or any non-allowed MIME type should be rejected by the Multer file filter with HTTP 400 'Invalid file type'.
Auth test: Calling the endpoint without a Bearer token should return HTTP 401.

item.name: Upload ID Document - Email Not Verified (403)

header.description: Use a token for a user whose email is NOT verified

request.description: Auth test: A user whose email has not been verified should receive HTTP 403 from the `requireVerified` middleware.

exec: pm.test('isIdVerified reset to false after re-upload', () => {

request.description: Re-upload scenario: A user who previously uploaded a document (even one that was already admin-verified) uploads a new image. The endpoint returns 200, updates `idDocumentUrl`, and **resets** `isIdVerified` to `false` — the admin must re-verify.

Soft-deletes the authenticated user's account. Sets deletedAt timestamp, invalidates all sessions (refresh tokens), removes device tokens, and marks active listings as DELETED. Blocked if the user has active trades (PENDING/ACCEPTED) or points in escrow.

exec: pm.test('Blocked when user has active trades', () => {

request.description: Expects 400 when the user has PENDING or ACCEPTED trades.
Expects 401 when no Authorization header is provided.

query.description: Filter by listing type: ITEM_OFFERING, ITEM_NEED, SERVICE_OFFERING, SERVICE_NEED
Filter by intent: OFFERING (items/services offered) or NEED (items/services needed)
Filter by price mode: POINTS, BARTER, BOTH

exec: console.log('tradeId set from listing trades:', pm.response.json().trades[0].id);

request.description:
Get all trades for a specific listing. Only returns trades where the authenticated user is a participant (buyer or seller). Non-participants receive an empty trades array with zero total — no 403 error.

**Supported query params:**
- `page` (int, default 1)
- `limit` (int, default 20)
- `status` (PENDING | ACCEPTED | REJECTED | COMPLETED | CANCELLED) — optional filter

**Authorization rules:**
- Trade party → sees their own trade(s) on this listing
- Non-party → `{ success: true, trades: [], pagination: { total: 0, ... } }`
- Unauthenticated → 401

item.name: Get Trades by Listing - Filter by Status

request.description: Same as 'Get Trades by Listing' but filtered to PENDING trades only. Useful for showing the listing owner which offers are still awaiting action. Change `status` to ACCEPTED, COMPLETED, REJECTED, or CANCELLED as needed.

request.description: Verifies that a user who is NOT a party in any trade on this listing silently receives an empty result (trades: []) rather than a 403 error. Use `{{userToken}}` set to a different user's access token.

Instantly purchase a listing at its listed price without negotiation. Creates a trade with ACCEPTED status. Points are escrowed immediately. Both parties must still confirm delivery. Message is optional - can send empty body {}.
Send a message in the trade chat. Available for PENDING, ACCEPTED, and COMPLETED (read-only) trades. Blocked for CANCELLED trades. Maximum content length: 2000 characters.

item.name: Chat - Send Message (Empty Content - Validation Error)

request.description: Test validation: Empty message content should return 400 error.

item.name: Chat - Send Message (Content Too Long - Validation Error)

request.description: Test validation: Message content exceeding 2000 characters should return 400 error.
Test error handling: Sending message to non-existent trade should return 404 error.
Retrieve chat messages for a trade with pagination. Returns messages in reverse chronological order (newest first).
Test pagination: Get second page with 20 messages per page.

query.description: ISO timestamp - Get only messages after this time (for polling)

request.description: Polling mode: Fetch only new messages created after the specified timestamp. Useful for real-time chat updates.
Test error handling: Getting messages from non-existent trade should return 404 error.
Mark all unread messages from the other participant as read. Returns count of messages marked.

item.name: Chat - Mark Messages as Read (Trade Not Found)

request.description: Test error handling: Marking messages as read for non-existent trade should return 404 error.
Get the count of unread messages from the other participant for this specific trade.

item.name: Chat - Get Unread Count (Trade Not Found)

request.description: Test error handling: Getting unread count for non-existent trade should return 404 error.
Get unread message counts for all trades where the user is a participant. Returns array of {tradeId, unreadCount} objects.

Admin approves a user's identity by setting `isIdVerified = true`. **Requires** the user to have already uploaded an ID document via `POST /api/users/me/id-document` — will return 400 'User has not uploaded ID document yet' otherwise.

**Prerequisites:** Admin JWT + target user has `idDocumentUrl` set.

item.name: Verify User ID - No Document Uploaded (400)

request.description: Error test: Attempting to verify a user who has never uploaded an ID document. The admin service checks `idDocumentUrl` and returns HTTP 400.
Error test: Attempting to verify a user who is already `isIdVerified = true`. The admin service throws HTTP 400 to prevent duplicate verification.

exec: console.log('ID Document URL for review:', body.user.idDocumentUrl);

request.description: Admin retrieves full user details including `idDocumentUrl`, `isIdVerified`, `idVerifiedAt`, and `idVerifiedBy` before deciding to approve or reject the identity claim.
Admin manually marks a user's email as verified (e.g. for accounts created via OAuth). This unblocks the user from calling `POST /api/users/me/id-document` which requires `requireVerified`.

query.description: Filter by listing type: ITEM_OFFERING, ITEM_NEED, SERVICE_OFFERING, SERVICE_NEED
Filter by intent: OFFERING (items/services offered) or NEED (items/services needed)

item.name: 11. KYC Identity Verification Workflow
item.description:
Complete end-to-end workflow for user identity verification (KYC). Run the requests in order. Steps 1-3 are user actions; Steps 4-6 are admin actions.

**Flow overview:**
1. Register & log in
2. Verify email (prerequisite for ID upload)
3. Upload ID document → idDocumentUrl is set, isIdVerified = false
4. Admin logs in
5. Admin views user details to inspect the document URL
6. Admin approves → isIdVerified = true

request.description: KYC Step 1 — Register a new user. The `accessToken` and `userId` variables are auto-populated by the test script.

exec: // Save current user token before switching to admin

header.description: Set adminToken variable to an admin JWT before running this step

request.description:
KYC Step 2 — Admin manually marks the user's email as verified so the `requireVerified` middleware allows them to upload their ID document.

**Alternative:** Use the real `GET /api/auth/verify-email?token=...` link sent to the user's email.

exec: console.log('Document uploaded. Pending admin review at:', body.idDocumentUrl);

formdata.description: Attach your ID image here (JPEG/PNG/WebP, max 5MB)

request.description: KYC Step 3 — Authenticated user uploads their government-issued ID. On success, `idDocumentUrl` is saved to the collection variable and the user is in **pending verification** state.

KYC Step 4 — Admin fetches full user details. The response includes `idDocumentUrl` (the uploaded image link), `isIdVerified: false`, and `idVerifiedAt: null`. Admin opens the URL to visually inspect the document.

KYC Step 5 — Admin approves the user's identity. Sets `isIdVerified = true`, `idVerifiedAt = now`, `idVerifiedBy = adminId`.

**Will fail (400) if:** user has no `idDocumentUrl` or is already verified.

item.name: Step 6 - User: Confirm isIdVerified in Profile

exec: pm.test('isIdVerified is true in user profile', () => {
console.log('KYC flow complete. User is now identity-verified.');

request.description: KYC Step 6 — User fetches their own profile to confirm `isIdVerified: true`. This is the final step of the KYC flow.

item.name: Edge Case - Admin Login (Get Admin Token)

exec: console.log('Admin token saved as adminToken variable');

request.description: Run this before Steps 2, 4, and 5 to get an admin token. The token is saved to the `adminToken` collection variable. Steps 2/4/5 use `{{adminToken}}` in their Authorization header.

item.name: 12. Get Trades by Listing Workflow
item.description:
End-to-end workflow demonstrating the GET /api/listings/:id/trades endpoint.

**Scenario:** Two users exist. User A creates a listing. User B makes a trade offer on it. The workflow shows:
1. User B (trade party) hits the endpoint → sees their trade
2. A different user (non-party) hits the same endpoint → gets an empty result
3. Status filter narrows results

Run requests in order. `listingId` and `tradeId` collection variables are auto-populated by earlier test scripts.

item.name: Step 1 - Login as User A (Listing Owner)

exec: console.log('User A logged in, accessToken set');

request.description: Login as User A who owns the listing. accessToken and userId variables are auto-set.

item.name: Step 2 - User A Creates a Listing

body.raw:
{
  "type": "ITEM_OFFERING",
  "categoryId": "{{categoryId}}",
  "title": "Vintage Camera",
  "description": "A working vintage film camera in great condition.",
  "condition": "Good",
  "location": "Manila, PH",
  "priceMode": "POINTS",
  "pricePoints": 200
}

request.description: User A creates a listing. The `listingId` variable is auto-set from the response.

item.name: Step 3 - Login as User B (Trade Offerer)

exec: console.log('User B logged in, accessToken and userToken set');

request.description: Login as User B who will make a trade offer. Both `accessToken` and `userToken` are set to User B's token.

item.name: Step 4 - User B Creates Trade Offer on Listing

request.description: User B makes a points trade offer on User A's listing. The `tradeId` is auto-set.

item.name: Step 5 - User B Gets Trades by Listing (Party — Sees Trade)

request.description: User B (buyer in the trade) calls GET /api/listings/:id/trades. Since they are a party, they see their own trade on this listing. Expects at least 1 trade in the response.

item.name: Step 6 - User B Gets Trades by Listing - Filter PENDING

request.description: Same endpoint with `?status=PENDING` filter. Verifies that only PENDING trades are returned.

item.name: Step 7 - Login as Unrelated User C

request.description: Login as User C who has NO trade on this listing. accessToken is updated to User C's token.

item.name: Step 8 - User C Gets Trades by Listing (Non-Party — Expects Empty)

header.description: User C's token — not a party in any trade on this listing

request.description: User C has no trade on this listing. They should receive HTTP 200 with an empty trades array and total = 0. No 403 is raised — access denial is silent.
Verify that an unauthenticated request (no Authorization header) returns HTTP 401.
